name: American Broadcast Employees
description_short: Federal credit union serving entertainment industry employees and northeastern residents with savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loan products.
description_long:
American Broadcast Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1967 and is headquartered in Rockville Centre, New York. As an NCUA-insured institution, it operates as a member-owned cooperative with approximately $113.63 million in assets. The credit union has maintained a 50+ year history serving a specific membership base in the media and entertainment sector.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), mortgages, home equity products, auto loans, and personal loans. According to their profile on BestCashCow, they offer competitive rates such as 4.20% APY on savings, 4.59% on 1-year CDs, and mortgage rates around 4.91% for 30-year fixed terms. Members can access traditional banking services through their physical branch location and can potentially access additional services through the credit union network.

American Broadcast Employees distinguishes itself through highly specialized membership eligibility. The credit union primarily serves employees of major entertainment companies including Walt Disney Company, ABC and its subsidiaries, Marvel Entertainment, ESPN, and over 20 other entertainment industry-related companies. Additionally, membership is available to residents of seven northeastern states (New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, Vermont, Connecticut, and Rhode Island) through joining the American and New York Consumer Council, making it more accessible than purely employer-based credit unions.

The credit union represents a solid option for eligible members seeking NCUA insurance protection and potential member benefits, though the website indicates incomplete rate information availability and limited digital transparency. The $113.63 million asset size places it as a small-to-mid-sized credit union, which may result in fewer branch locations and potentially less advanced digital banking features compared to larger national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts with competitive APY rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with terms from 1-5 years
Physical branch banking at Rockville Centre, NY location
pros: NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000
Membership available to entertainment industry employees at major studios (Disney, ABC, ESPN, Marvel)
Geographic membership option for northeastern residents via American and New York Consumer Council
Competitive deposit rates (4.20% savings APY, 4.59% 1-year CD rate as of April 2026)
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, HELOCs, auto loans, and personal loans
Established 50+ year history since 1967 with $113.63 million in assets
Member-owned cooperative structure with potential for member benefits and lower fees
cons: Limited branch locations with single headquarters listed in Rockville Centre, NY
Incomplete rate transparency on website with many products showing 'N.A.' for rate comparisons
Smaller institution size may mean fewer digital banking features or less advanced technology
Restrictive membership requirements limiting accessibility compared to community banks
Limited online presence and outdated website information (rates not current for all products)
best_for: Entertainment industry employees at Disney, ABC, ESPN, Marvel, and affiliated companies
Northeastern residents in NY, NJ, PA, MA, VT, CT, or RI seeking credit union membership
Members prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and member-owned cooperative benefits
Customers seeking relationship banking through a specialized industry credit union
diagnosis: American Broadcast Employees Federal Credit Union is best for entertainment industry employees and northeastern U.S. residents meeting membership criteria who value NCUA protection and member-owned cooperative benefits. The main caveat is its small size and limited digital presence, requiring prospective members to contact directly for current rates and complete product information, as the website lacks updated rate transparency.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
